channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and More Red Carpet Looks from “The Devil Wears Prada 2”
item.description: At a starry premiere at Lincoln Center, fashion industry figures reflected on just how much the original film got right.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Lanna Apisukh
item.media:description: Stars of the “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” Meryl Streep, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci and Anne Hathaway, pose at the film’s premiere at Lincoln Center in Manhattan.

item.title: How ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Red Carpet Looks Came Together
item.description: The press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2” has been a style spectacle for its stars and their stylists, who have relied on group chats, sweatpants and camaraderie to pull it off.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: Why Fashion Suddenly Loves Older Women
item.description: Representation of women of a certain age has never been higher. What’s going on?
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: This Design Duo Loves a Challenge
item.description: When tastemakers are having a décor emergency, they bring in Adam Charlap Hyman and Andre Herrero.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:credit: Evelyn Freja for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andre Herrero, left, and Adam Charlap Hyman are the latest winners of the annual Interior Design award overseen by the Cooper Hewitt Smithsonian Design Museum.

item.title: Designing for Mother Mary, a Fictional Pop Star Who Happens to Be Anne Hathaway
item.description: A costume designer and a couturier teamed up to create ethereal looks for Ms. Hathaway’s titular pop star in the new film “Mother Mary.”
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore

item.title: Gwendoline Riley Would Prefer You Resist Assuming Her Life Is Like Her Books
item.description: The British author Gwendoline Riley may be as emotionally guarded as the women in her novels, which have caught on in America.
item.dc:creator: Thessaly La Force
item.media:credit: Billy Barraclough for The New York Times
item.media:description: The writer Gwendoline Riley in London ahead of the publication of her new novel, “The Palm House.”

item.title: Activists Urge Boycott of ‘Bezos Met Gala’ With Posters Around NYC
item.description: A guerrilla activist group is covering New York with posters criticizing the billionaire Jeff Bezos’ involvement in the event, a fund-raiser for the Metropolitan Museum.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

item.title: Mamdani Says He Plans to Skip the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: In an interview with the local news outfit Hell Gate, the New York mayor framed his decision to avoid the glamorous fund-raiser as a way to keep his focus on affordability.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani might have seemed out of place at the Met Gala, an event that is widely considered to be the playground of the rich and famous.

item.title: The Nerve Center of This Art Fair Isn’t Painting. It’s Couture.
item.description: The Independent fair will push boundaries, featuring designs by Rei Kawakubo and Comme des Garçons that blur the line between fashion and sculpture.
item.dc:creator: Tim F. Schneider

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: Mapping happiness, Monday dinners and the rise of the scapebear.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Even Our Waitress Was Bored’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: A Rabbi Talks Torah on TikTok
item.description: Rabbi Shalom Landau has found a large new audience online, some of which isn’t Jewish. He sticks to ancient wisdom and hopes for the best.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rabbi Shalom Landau at his desk in the Williamsburg neighborhood of Brooklyn.

item.title: My Parents Are Obsessed With Food and Weight. Help!
item.description: A reader fears that her parents’ decades-long food fixation could jeopardize her efforts to keep her own eating disorder under control.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Aging in Place: How Technology Might Help You Grow Old at Home
item.description: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Susan Shain

item.title: How Tim Cook’s Tech Uniform Helped Make Apple Trillions
item.description: The unassuming look of the outgoing Apple chief executive was an asset, as he navigated pop culture, the president and following Steve Jobs.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: In his navy polos and dark jeans, Mr. Cook became a recognizable but unassuming figurehead of Apple. It may have been an asset for him to present as a kind of blank slate.

item.title: Armani Goes Back to the Archive
item.description: A new collection dusts off decades-old creations, plus a dispatch from a Brunello Cucinelli movie premiere and the Political Tie Tracker.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Airy Beachy Clothes
item.description: A couple stood out in Upper Manhattan for wearing billowy attire the colors of water and sand.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Met Gala 2026 Guide: Theme, Hosts, Dress Code and More
item.description: What to know about this year’s celebration, including the return of a superstar last seen on the Met carpet 10 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Beyoncé arriving to the Met Gala in 2016, the last year she attended the event.

item.title: His Watch Brand Is ‘Doing It The Traditional Way’
item.description: Love Hunter has been drawn to timepieces since he was a child. Now, after years of selling and repairing them, it’s time for his own creations to take center stage.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder and Tony Cenicola
item.category: Smith, Alfred E, Career and Technical Education High School
item.media:description: “This was always the goal, to create something with my own hands,” said Love Hunter, with his son Mick, at his watchmaking shop in Derby, Conn.

item.title: With These Watches, It’s Not Just What’s on the Inside That Counts
item.description: A look at the new skeleton watch models unveiled by Hermès, Piaget and Panerai.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher

item.title: They Put Their Tool Watches to the Test
item.description: For these wearers of rugged timepieces, form follows function.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Colston
item.media:credit: Morten Wanvik for The New York Times
item.media:description: Morten Paulsen during transit aboard the anchor handler Island Valiant, wearing the Marathon Jumbo Diver’s Quartz JSAR 46mm, the watch he said he relied on most during offshore operations.

item.title: Luxury Brands Bet on the Middle East. War Has Damaged Their Plans.
item.description: High-end brands such as Louis Vuitton and Hermès are looking to other regions as sales in Persian Gulf nations plummet.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SA
item.media:description: A Louis Vuitton store in Paris. LVMH has seen a drop in sales in its Middle Eastern stores, with demand falling across the gulf region.

item.title: What ‘Summer House’ Reveals About the Fallout From a Friend Dating Your Ex
item.description: From reality TV to real life, the emotional fallout of a friend dating an ex can last long after a relationship ends.
item.dc:creator: Martine Thompson

item.title: The Latest Trend in Wedding Fashion: The Bouquet ‘Purse’
item.description: Once popular in the Victorian era, flower “purses,” crafted of faux and real flowers, are being carried down the aisle with a handle or grasped like a clutch.
item.dc:creator: Perri Ormont Blumberg

item.title: Tackling the Trust Factor for Secondhand Watches
item.description: Online start-ups aim to clarify pricing and authenticity for pre-owned and vintage watches.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky
item.media:credit: Beth Coller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Quaid Walker, chief executive of Bezel, an online marketplace for pre-owned luxury watches.

item.title: For the British Watch Brand Bremont, the Next Stop Is the Moon
item.description: The billionaire Bill Ackman’s investment in the company might not yet have vastly altered its fortunes, but he’s concerned with more than earthly riches.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: With Every Watch, This Collector Revels in the Story
item.description: Jay Liu shares some of his favorite spots in Tokyo, along with his reasons for collecting vintage timepieces.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Morelli and James Whitlow Delano
item.media:description: The watch collector Jay Liu wearing a vintage Rolex Daytona at the Spiral building in Tokyo.

item.title: Chanel Takes a Leading Role in Producing Ceramic Watches
item.description: The fashion house has expanded its signature line, the J12, which is now offered in three colors and new sizes.
item.dc:creator: Ming Liu
item.media:credit: Joann Pai for The New York Times
item.media:description: Frédéric Grangié, president of Chanel watches and fine jewelry. “By watchmaker standards, we are very, very young,” he said. “But if I look at the number of creations, novelties and unexpected designs that we brought over the course of 40 years, it is probably more than most.”

item.title: Universal Genève Returns to the Watch World With New Models
item.description: The brand has undergone a two-year rebuilding after it was acquired by Breitling in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: At Tiffany, Watch Design Inspired by Its Own Jewelry Icons
item.description: The house has translated its paillonné enamel bangle, once favored by Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis, into a limited-edition timepiece.
item.dc:creator: Milena Lazazzera

item.title: These Watches Are Personal, Unique and Made to Keep
item.description: For the ultimate in high-level luxury, buyers are commissioning timepieces that are special enough to be passed down through generations.
item.dc:creator: Carol Besler

item.title: A Dance Party for Martha Graham With Ariana DeBose and Padma Lakshmi
item.description: Stars and luminaries honored the dance company’s 100th anniversary with dinner, performances and a champagne toast at the New York Public Library.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Rebecca Smeyne
item.category: Graham, Martha, Center of Contemporary Dance
New York Public Library for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Rebecca Smeyne for The New York Times

item.title: Paul Waldman, Creator and Curator of Art Museum for Birds, Dies at 89
item.description: A dyslexic teenager, he reinvented himself as a bodybuilder. Then he turned to art, producing transgressive paintings and elaborate birdhouses.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald
item.media:credit: Michael Shavel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Paul Waldman in 1993 with the International Bird Museum that he built on the roof of his house in Southampton, N.Y. The multistory birdhouse had an art gallery that displayed miniature works by Roy Lichtenstein and others.

item.title: In Trump’s Orbit, Women Aren’t the Only Ones Concerned About Their Looks
item.description: In Trump world, the male ego is often evident — and their appearance scrutinized — under a president’s gaze.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.media:description: For all the talk about Mar-a-Lago-inspired cosmetic surgery for women in Trump’s world, the attention paid to, and the efforts to safeguard, the male ego also stand out. Many of the men in his administration have adopted a signature look.

item.title: Wedding Menus Swapping Chicken and Steak for Burgers and Fries
item.description: Tired of traditional wedding fare? Some couples are opting for elevated versions of familiar comfort foods, ketchup included.
item.dc:creator: Ellen O’Brien

item.title: Barber Osgerby, a British Design Team, Is Honored in Milan
item.description: The Triennale design museum is hosting an exhibition of work by the renowned studio Barber Osgerby.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Goldberg

item.title: At Coachella, the Bob Baker Marionette Theater Makes Its Debut
item.description: After years of struggling to keep the doors open, the Bob Baker Marionette Theater joined a lineup that included Justin Bieber and Katseye.
item.dc:creator: Juno Carmel
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:description: Alien puppets rehearsing the song “Soda Pop” from “KPop Demon Hunters.”

item.title: Before They Met, Their Parents Had Already Known Each Other
item.description: When Emily Wiser first met Griffin Lear, she was excited to tell her father that she was dating a Dartmouth grad like him. Then they shared some history of their own about Mr. Lear’s parents.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Painting Over My Painful Past
item.description: My father treated me more like a wife than a child. It took me decades to face the truth of what happened.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Hanssen

item.title: A Duet Decades in the Making
item.description: After meeting for a website project in 2020, James Squires and Jacqui Agostinelli bonded over their love of music, forming both a marriage and their musical duo, Sweet Basil.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Her Grandmother Told Her ‘Maybe You’ll Marry Him One Day’
item.description: After Ben Sweeney and Lauren Fogel debarked their plane at 3 a.m., he invited her to stay at his parents’ house nearby. Her grandmother was impressed.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: It Took a Persistent Friend and a Dave Matthews Concert to Seal the Deal
item.description: Daniel Martinez Clavijo and Johanna Mele finally met in 2021, thanks to their mutual friend, who had been trying to set them up for almost a decade, and a chance encounter at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: On ‘The Pitt,’ the Lingering Effects of Trauma Take the Spotlight
item.description: On top of the daily toll of treating patients, the show’s medical providers bring their own scars to the E.R.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: What Heelys, the Sneakers With Wheels, Taught Me About Momentum as a Child
item.description: Heelys, the sneakers with wheels that were a fad in the 2000s, helped me form my first friendships. Recently, I decided to tap into that joy again.
item.dc:creator: Ash Wu
item.media:description: Ash Wu wearing a pair of Heelys. Reconnecting with the shoes brought back memories of her childhood.

item.title: Roger Adams Dies at 71; Invented the Rolling Sneakers Known as Heelys
item.description: You could walk in them like gym shoes, but if you rocked back on your heels the wheels emerged, turning them into roller skates. In the early 2000s, the company sold millions.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: What We Know About Clavicular’s Apparent Overdose and Hospitalization
item.description: A harrowing incident involving Clavicular, ambassador to the “looksmaxxing” community, was captured on the same platforms that made him a star.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
item.media:description: Braden Peters, better known as Clavicular, was carried out of a bar in Miami this week after an apparent overdose. Not long after, he was back out on the town for a club event.

item.title: What We Lose When Everything Is ‘-Coded’
item.description: On the social internet, our fascination with analyzing the hidden messages in our culture has been flattened into one word.
item.dc:creator: Dan Brooks

item.title: Aging in a Brightly Lit, Big City: Jay McInerney, With a New Novel
item.description: In 1984, Jay McInerney was a famous, young, hedonistic novelist. Now 71, he is wistful as he wraps up his tetralogy about a couple whose city, and marriage, are tested by the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: David Daley
item.category: See You on the Other Side (Book)
item.media:credit: Gus Powell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jay McInerney, author of “See You on the Other Side,” at the Odeon restaurant in TriBeCa.

item.title: How Do You Decide Which Clothes Are Worth the Price?
item.description: Tell us about how you decide to spend your money and what is worth the splurge.
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore
